checks.expected: From Case Retrieval to Normative Material Screening
A Proof-Carrying Audit Protocol for Legal AI Recommendation Systems
The evaluation is not a retrieval leaderboard
VQ1 & The status function is decorative or under-specified
VQ2 & The rule works only on constructed examples
VQ3 & A simpler rule explains the results
VQ4 & Results are brittle to coding, architecture, contestation or certificate manipulation
Worked application: Loper Bright evidence ladder
Recall 1.00 and F1 0.97 do not create screening status
A screening output can enter reasons only through accountable adoption
- 8,023,761 validation operations, with scenario rows, generated counterfactuals, finite-state edges, substitute-rule predictions and replay checks reported separately
- 466,560 high-status claim-attempt states, 3,499,200 cover edges and 3,732,480 substitute-rule predictions
- 1019/1019 status-lattice high-status necessity checks and 1019/1019 gate-ablation drops passed
- 1,000 metric bootstrap resamples and 1,000 metric permutation shuffles
